question: Describe the primary function of the Control Unit (CU) within the CPU and explain how it directs the operation of the other main components.
expectedPoints: The Control Unit is the component that directs the operation of the processor.
It interprets (fetches and decodes) the instructions of a program.
It generates the timing and control signals that coordinate the movement of data through the CPU and between the CPU and other components (like memory and I/O).

question: List and briefly define the four main structural components of a general-purpose computer as described by the von Neumann architecture.
expectedPoints: Central Processing Unit (CPU): The component that executes instructions.
Main Memory: Stores data and programs that are currently in use (volatile).
I/O Systems: The mechanisms for getting data into and out of the computer.
System Interconnection: The bus structure that provides communication between the CPU, memory, and I/O.

question: Explain the complete fetch-decode-execute cycle, detailing the role of key registers (like MAR, MBR, PC, IR) and the sequence of operations for a single instruction.
expectedPoints: Fetch: The address of the next instruction is loaded from the Program Counter (PC) into the Memory Address Register (MAR). The instruction is fetched from memory into the Memory Buffer Register (MBR) and then transferred to the Instruction Register (IR). The PC is then incremented.
Decode: The control unit decodes the instruction in the IR to determine the operation to be performed and the operands required.

Execute: The control unit generates the necessary signals to perform the operation. This may involve reading operands from registers or memory, performing an arithmetic/logic operation in the ALU, and writing a result back to a register or memory.
The cycle then repeats for the next instruction.
